Celine Nurhidayah Santoso (24031554033) | Dista Ishlahul Amalia (24031554063) | Dessy Saputri (24031554190)

2026-04-26

##  Max.   : 3.700   Max.   : 3.510000
dim(data)
## [1] 4424   37

FEATURE SELECTION

df <- data[,c(
"Target",
"Age.at.enrollment",
"Admission.grade",
"Previous.qualification..grade.",
"Curricular.units.1st.sem..approved.",
"Curricular.units.2nd.sem..approved."
)]

df$Target <- as.factor(df$Target)
str(df)
## 'data.frame':    4424 obs. of  6 variables:
##  $ Target                             : Factor w/ 3 levels "Dropout","Enrolled",..: 1 3 1 3 3 3 3 1 3 1 ...
##  $ Age.at.enrollment                  : int  20 19 19 20 45 50 18 22 21 18 ...
##  $ Admission.grade                    : num  127 142 125 120 142 ...
##  $ Previous.qualification..grade.     : num  122 160 122 122 100 ...
##  $ Curricular.units.1st.sem..approved.: int  0 6 0 6 5 5 7 0 6 5 ...
##  $ Curricular.units.2nd.sem..approved.: int  0 6 0 5 6 5 8 0 6 2 ...

STANDARDISASI

X <- scale(
df[,-1]
)

df_scaled <- data.frame(
Target=df$Target,
X
)

VISUALISASI AWAL

# Distribusi kelas Target
ggplot(
df_scaled,
aes(Target, fill=Target)
)+
geom_bar()+
ggtitle(
"Distribusi Status Akademik"
)

# Boxplot per Kelas
ggplot(
df_scaled,
aes(
Target,
Admission.grade,
fill=Target
)
)+
geom_boxplot()+
ggtitle(
"Admission Grade per Status"
)

# Pairplot
ggpairs(
df_scaled,
aes(color=Target)
)
## `stat_bin()` using `bins = 30`. Pick better value `binwidth`.
## `stat_bin()` using `bins = 30`. Pick better value `binwidth`.
## `stat_bin()` using `bins = 30`. Pick better value `binwidth`.
## `stat_bin()` using `bins = 30`. Pick better value `binwidth`.
## `stat_bin()` using `bins = 30`. Pick better value `binwidth`.

# Heatmap Korelasi
cor_matrix <- cor(
df_scaled[,-1]
)

corrplot(
cor_matrix,
method="color",
addCoef.col="black"
)

## UJI DEPENDENSI (MULTIKOLINEARITAS)

# VIF
model_vif <- lm(
  Admission.grade ~ 
  Age.at.enrollment +
  Previous.qualification..grade. +
  Curricular.units.1st.sem..approved. +
  Curricular.units.2nd.sem..approved.,
  data = df_scaled
)

vif(model_vif)
##                   Age.at.enrollment      Previous.qualification..grade. 
##                            1.037861                            1.014419 
## Curricular.units.1st.sem..approved. Curricular.units.2nd.sem..approved. 
##                            5.543513                            5.595931

ANALISIS DISKRIMINAN

# Uji Normalitas
MVN::mvn(
  df_scaled[,-1],
  mvn_test = "hz"
)
## $multivariate_normality
##            Test Statistic p.value     Method          MVN
## 1 Henze-Zirkler    62.305  <0.001 asymptotic ✗ Not normal
## 
## $univariate_normality
##               Test                            Variable Statistic p.value
## 1 Anderson-Darling                   Age.at.enrollment   470.094  <0.001
## 2 Anderson-Darling                     Admission.grade    20.391  <0.001
## 3 Anderson-Darling      Previous.qualification..grade.    29.303  <0.001
## 4 Anderson-Darling Curricular.units.1st.sem..approved.   142.968  <0.001
## 5 Anderson-Darling Curricular.units.2nd.sem..approved.   113.301  <0.001
##      Normality
## 1 ✗ Not normal
## 2 ✗ Not normal
## 3 ✗ Not normal
## 4 ✗ Not normal
## 5 ✗ Not normal
## 
## $descriptives

## 2 -0.627 0.540 0.530    3.661
## 3 -0.577 0.560 0.313    3.966
## 4 -0.552 0.418 0.766    6.092
## 5 -0.808 0.519 0.306    3.843

## 4408                          0.41800264                           0.5188450
## 4409                          0.09482129                           0.5188450
## 4410                          0.09482129                           0.1871441
## 4411                          0.41800264                           0.1871441
## 4412                          0.41800264                          -0.4762578
## 4413                         -0.22836006                           0.1871441
## 4414                          0.09482129                          -0.8079587
## 4415                          0.09482129                           0.1871441
## 4416                         -0.55154140                          -0.4762578
## 4417                          2.35709072                           2.5090506
## 4418                          0.41800264                           0.5188450
## 4419                          0.41800264                           0.8505460
## 4420                          0.09482129                           0.1871441
## 4421                          0.41800264                          -0.8079587
## 4422                          0.74118399                          -1.1396596
## 4423                          0.09482129                           0.1871441
## 4424                          0.41800264                           0.5188450
## 
## $subset
## NULL
## 
## $outlierMethod
## [1] "none"
## 
## attr(,"class")
## [1] "mvn"
# Box's M
boxM(
df_scaled[,-1],
df_scaled$Target
)
## 
##  Box's M-test for Homogeneity of Covariance Matrices
## 
## data:  df_scaled[, -1]
## Chi-Sq (approx.) = 650.15, df = 30, p-value < 2.2e-16
# Split Data
set.seed(123)
index <- sample(
1:nrow(df_scaled),
0.7*nrow(df_scaled)
)

train <- df_scaled[index,]
test <- df_scaled[-index,]
# LDA
fit_lda <- lda(
Target~.,
data=train
)

fit_lda
## Call:
## lda(Target ~ ., data = train)
## 
## Prior probabilities of groups:
##   Dropout  Enrolled  Graduate 
## 0.3191214 0.1828165 0.4980620 
## 
## Group means:
##          Age.at.enrollment Admission.grade Previous.qualification..grade.
## Dropout         0.36829729      -0.1368222                     -0.1084456
## Enrolled       -0.07964971      -0.1302598                     -0.1196630
## Graduate       -0.18767315       0.1344950                      0.1179102
##          Curricular.units.1st.sem..approved.
## Dropout                           -0.6784588
## Enrolled                          -0.1175877
## Graduate                           0.4968069
##          Curricular.units.2nd.sem..approved.
## Dropout                          -0.81265891
## Enrolled                         -0.09943143
## Graduate                          0.57929119
## 
## Coefficients of linear discriminants:
##                                            LD1        LD2
## Age.at.enrollment                   -0.3362552  0.6494575
## Admission.grade                      0.1344681  0.3591485
## Previous.qualification..grade.       0.0376309  0.4193250
## Curricular.units.1st.sem..approved. -0.3416258  0.7457136
## Curricular.units.2nd.sem..approved.  1.5234119 -0.5179817
## 
## Proportion of trace:
##    LD1    LD2 
## 0.9889 0.0111
fit_lda$scaling
##                                            LD1        LD2
## Age.at.enrollment                   -0.3362552  0.6494575
## Admission.grade                      0.1344681  0.3591485
## Previous.qualification..grade.       0.0376309  0.4193250
## Curricular.units.1st.sem..approved. -0.3416258  0.7457136
## Curricular.units.2nd.sem..approved.  1.5234119 -0.5179817
# Prediksi
pred_lda <- predict(
fit_lda,
test
)
# Confussion Matrix
cm <- table(
Prediksi=pred_lda$class,
Aktual=test$Target
)

cm
##           Aktual
## Prediksi   Dropout Enrolled Graduate
##   Dropout      334       93       54
##   Enrolled       0        0        0
##   Graduate      99      135      613
mean(
pred_lda$class==test$Target
)
## [1] 0.7131024
#Visualisasi
cm_df <- as.data.frame(cm)

ggplot(
cm_df,
aes(
Aktual,
Prediksi,
fill=Freq
)
)+
geom_tile()+
geom_text(
aes(label=Freq)
)+
ggtitle(
"Confusion Matrix LDA"
)

# Plot Canonical Discriminant
lda_values <- predict(
fit_lda
)$x
plot(
lda_values,
col=as.numeric(train$Target),
pch=19,
main="Discriminant Function Plot"
)

## MULTINOMIAL LOGISTIC

# Multinomial Berganda
fit_full <- multinom(
Target~
Age.at.enrollment+
Admission.grade+
Previous.qualification..grade.+
Curricular.units.1st.sem..approved.+
Curricular.units.2nd.sem..approved.,
data=df_scaled
)
## # weights:  21 (12 variable)
## initial  value 4860.260765 
## iter  10 value 3951.020229
## iter  20 value 3301.190658
## final  value 3301.165041 
## converged
summary(
fit_full
)
## Call:
## multinom(formula = Target ~ Age.at.enrollment + Admission.grade + 
##     Previous.qualification..grade. + Curricular.units.1st.sem..approved. + 
##     Curricular.units.2nd.sem..approved., data = df_scaled)
## 
## Coefficients:
##          (Intercept) Age.at.enrollment Admission.grade
## Enrolled -0.01950758        -0.4150483      0.05984933
## Graduate  0.69274506        -0.5498639      0.24479767
##          Previous.qualification..grade. Curricular.units.1st.sem..approved.
## Enrolled                    -0.01426110                          -0.2260134
## Graduate                     0.08255453                          -0.2668455
##          Curricular.units.2nd.sem..approved.
## Enrolled                            1.255554
## Graduate                            2.516379
## 
## Std. Errors:
##          (Intercept) Age.at.enrollment Admission.grade
## Enrolled  0.05587048        0.05022936      0.05870802
## Graduate  0.05064882        0.04786196      0.05730785
##          Previous.qualification..grade. Curricular.units.1st.sem..approved.
## Enrolled                     0.05915241                           0.1150957
## Graduate                     0.05672743                           0.1139353
##          Curricular.units.2nd.sem..approved.
## Enrolled                           0.1172860
## Graduate                           0.1192341
## 
## Residual Deviance: 6602.33 
## AIC: 6626.33
# Rasio Risiko Relatif
exp(
coef(fit_full)
)
##          (Intercept) Age.at.enrollment Admission.grade
## Enrolled   0.9806815         0.6603084        1.061677
## Graduate   1.9991959         0.5770283        1.277363
##          Previous.qualification..grade. Curricular.units.1st.sem..approved.
## Enrolled                      0.9858401                           0.7977074
## Graduate                      1.0860579                           0.7657914
##          Curricular.units.2nd.sem..approved.
## Enrolled                            3.509781
## Graduate                           12.383679
# Marginal Effects
avg_comparisons(
fit_full,
variables=
"Admission.grade",
type="probs"
)
## 
##     Group Estimate Std. Error     z Pr(>|z|)    S   2.5 %   97.5 %
##  Dropout   -0.0206    0.00617 -3.34   <0.001 10.2 -0.0327 -0.00854
##  Enrolled  -0.0119    0.00702 -1.69   0.0909  3.5 -0.0256  0.00189
##  Graduate   0.0325    0.00760  4.28   <0.001 15.7  0.0176  0.04739
## 
## Term: Admission.grade
## Type: probs
## Comparison: +1
# Pseudo R2
pR2(
fit_full
)
## fitting null model for pseudo-r2
## # weights:  6 (2 variable)
## initial  value 4860.260765 
## final  value 4511.832789 
## converged
##           llh       llhNull            G2      McFadden          r2ML 
## -3301.1650412 -4511.8327885  2421.3354947     0.2683317     0.4215008 
##          r2CU 
##     0.4845209
# Prediksi
pred_multi <- predict(
fit_full
)

table(
Prediksi=pred_multi,
Aktual=df_scaled$Target
)
##           Aktual
## Prediksi   Dropout Enrolled Graduate
##   Dropout     1093      310      163
##   Enrolled       0        0        0
##   Graduate     328      484     2046
mean(
pred_multi==
df_scaled$Target
)
## [1] 0.7095389
# Visual Probabilitas Prediksi
prob <- predict(
fit_full,
type="probs"
)

prob_df <- as.data.frame(
prob
)

head(prob_df)
##      Dropout  Enrolled   Graduate
## 1 0.73952273 0.1952575 0.06521978
## 2 0.05845569 0.1307854 0.81075890
## 3 0.73157897 0.2019231 0.06649792
## 4 0.18794077 0.2488128 0.56324639
## 5 0.31560433 0.1946858 0.48970982
## 6 0.61178656 0.1635603 0.22465313
ggplot(
prob_df,
aes(Graduate)
)+
geom_histogram(
bins=30
)+
ggtitle(
"Probabilitas Prediksi Graduate"
)
